Name: BundleType
Title: BundleType
Description: Indicates the purpose of a bundle - how it is intended to be used.

Name: DiagnosisRole
Title: DiagnosisRole
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to express the role of a diagnosis on the Encounter or EpisodeOfCare record.

Name: AdmitSource
Title: Admit source
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate from where the patient came in.

Name: Diet
Title: Diet
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate dietary preferences or restrictions a patient may have.

Name: DischargeDisposition
Title: Discharge disposition
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to where the patient left the hospital.

Name: EncounterLocationStatus
Title: EncounterLocationStatus
Description: The status of the location.

Name: ParticipantType
Title: Participant type
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate how an individual participates in an encounter.

Name: EncounterReasonCodes
Title: Encounter Reason Codes
Description: This examples value set defines the set of codes that can be used to indicate reasons for an encounter.

Name: SpecialArrangements
Title: Special arrangements
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the kinds of special arrangements in place for a patients visit.

Name: SpecialCourtesy
Title: Special courtesy
Description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate special courtesies provided to the patient.

Name: EncounterStatus
Title: EncounterStatus
Description: Current state of the encounter.

Name: EncounterType
Title: Encounter type
Description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the type of encounter: a specific code indicating type of service provided.

Name: HTTPVerb
Title: HTTPVerb
Description: HTTP verbs (in the HTTP command line). See [HTTP rfc](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231) for details.

Name: CommonLanguages
Title: Common Languages
Description: This value set includes common codes from BCP-47 (http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47)

Name: LocationType
Title: Location type
Description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the physical form of the Location.

Name: ExampleMessageReasonCodes
Title: Example Message Reason Codes
Description: Example Message Reasons. These are the set of codes that might be used an updating an encounter using admin-update.

Name: ResourceType
Title: ResourceType
Description: One of the resource types defined as part of this version of FHIR.

Name: ResponseType
Title: ResponseType
Description: The kind of response to a message.

Name: SearchEntryMode
Title: SearchEntryMode
Description: Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

Name: ServiceType
Title: Service type
Description: This value set defines an example set of codes of service-types.

Name: MedComHospitalNotificationEncounterClass
Title: MedComHospitalNotificationEncounterClassCodes
Description: ValueSet containg class codes for hospital notification encounter

Name: MedComHospitalNotificationEncounterStatus
Title: MedComHospitalNotificationEncounterStatusCodes
Description: ValueSet containing status codes for hospital notification encounter

Name: MedComMessagingMessageTypes
Title: MedComMessagingMessageTypes
Description: All message types of MedCom messages

Name: Hl7VSReAdmissionIndicator
Title: hl7VS-re-admissionIndicator
Description: Value Set of codes which are used to specify that a patient is being re-admitted to a healthcare facility from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.

Name: ActEncounterCode
Title: ActEncounterCode
Description: Domain provides codes that qualify the ActEncounterClass (ENC)

Name: ActPriority
Title: ActPriority
Description: A code or set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency,) specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.    *Discussion:* This attribute is used in orders to indicate the ordered priority, and in event documentation it indicates the actual priority used to perform the act. In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.
